mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or anchored near zero, discount rates on equities stay compressed and risk appetite improves, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. This signal is falsified if rate cuts do not coincide with above-baseline forward equity gains.
failure_condition: If triggered by declining FEDFUNDS, 20-day forward SPX returns are negative in more than 55% of cases or the conditional average return is below the unconditional mean over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above recent levels signals weakening income growth and softer demand, which lowers expected corporate revenue and depresses S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect fails if unemployment spikes are met by positive equity performance.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject this hypothesis if, after UNRATE crosses the trigger, 20-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 60% of instances or the average return is not statistically lower than the unconditional benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS accelerates relative to its recent trend, household and retail demand are expanding, which lifts near-term revenue expectations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. The signal should fail when sales growth decelerates or turns negative.
failure_condition: If positive RSAFS trend periods do not produce higher 5-day SPX returns than negative trend periods, or if the rank correlation stays below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO indicates stronger industrial output and better near-term earnings breadth, which should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The relationship breaks if output gains occur without equity follow-through.
failure_condition: Reject if higher INDPRO trend regimes fail to outperform lower trend regimes on 60-day SPX returns, or if the conditional hit rate remains below 52% across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in HOUST and PAYEMS signals broad demand weakening: fewer housing starts reduce construction activity while slower payroll growth reduces consumer spending, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. Either series alone is weaker than the combined effect.
failure_condition: If the joint signal from falling HOUST and PAYEMS does not outperform each proxy alone, or if triggered instances do not generate negative 20-day SPX returns more often than 55% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of low CPIAUCSL inflation and elevated FEDFUNDS implies a restrictive real-rate regime that compresses valuation multiples and lowers S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. This mechanism is not supported if high rates are offset by clearly falling inflation and equities still rally.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS above 4.5% and CPIAUCSL inflation not accelerating
failure_condition: If restrictive real-rate episodes produce positive 60-day SPX returns more than 50% of the time, or if the joint CPIAUCSL-FEDFUNDS condition has no edge over FEDFUNDS alone.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle labor cooling phase, a rising UNRATE paired with slowing PAYEMS indicates weakening household income and lower future consumption, which should pressure S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. The effect is strongest when unemployment rises while payroll gains flatten.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises and PAYEMS growth slows over the same 2-month window
failure_condition: If the combined labor-deterioration trigger does not produce negative 5-day SPX returns in at least 55% of cases, or if the average return is not lower than when only one labor series weakens.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery, rising GDPC1 together with improving RSAFS signals a broad rebound in final demand and corporate revenue, which should generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The signal fails if GDP growth improves without sales confirmation and equities do not respond.
failure_condition: Reject if joint increases in GDPC1 and RSAFS do not outperform either series alone on 60-day SPX returns, or if the conditional return sign is not positive in more than 55% of matched episodes.
